2. Corrective Actions & Responsible Parties

This section is sequenced in governance order: escalation control first, operating path second, trigger rules third, then ownership and weekly execution duties.

2.1 Escalation Matrix Process

The escalation portion of the CAP should appear as a management control mechanism, not a plain table. The visual below converts the approved escalation matrix into a presentation-ready format for STC review while preserving the same reporting lines from the attached Middle Sea organization file.

How the Escalation Path Must Operate in Practice

1

Level One — Field Detection

Site Supervisor, Quality Supervisor / Engineer, Permit Task Owner, Designer, or Purchasing Task Owner records the issue, assigns owner, and starts immediate containment.

2

Level Two — Functional Response

Site Engineer, Project Manager, Permit Manager, Designer Manager, or Program/Projects Manager validates ownership, deadline, and action route based on the issue type.

3

Level Three — Management Control

Project Manager or HSEQ Director intervenes when violations recur, remain unresolved, or create district-level execution exposure requiring management correction.

4

Level Four — Executive Escalation

Program/Projects Manager takes over when recovery stalls, penalties intensify, or the issue creates direct STC, municipality, or delivery risk.

Presentation rule: when discussing the CAP with STC, show escalation as a timed control path: detect → assign → escalate → resolve. This demonstrates governance discipline, not only corrective intent.

Escalation Triggers That Must Be Applied

24-hour no-action triggerAny failed checkpoint not corrected within 24 hours moves from Level One to Level Two with a dated rectification order and photo-proof requirement.
Recurrence triggerRepeated occurrence of the same violation code in the same district escalates through Project Manager to HSEQ Director for preventive action review.
Backlog triggerIf the open backlog does not decline for two consecutive reviews, the matter is escalated to Program/Projects Manager for recovery direction.
Permit / design blocker triggerAny dependency blocking closure beyond the agreed milestone escalates through the permit or design route until an executable release is issued.
Critical STC exposure triggerAny high-risk municipality or client-facing issue is escalated same day to executive level with owner, containment action, and verified closure ETA.

Source note: the matrix keeps the original dynamic field roles from the attachment, including the note that Site Supervision allocation is dynamic and not fixed to one dedicated site.

2.2 How the Escalation Path Must Operate in Practice

Field detection / الرصد الميداني
Any violation identified during inspection must be logged the same day with district, exact issue text, photo evidence, and owner name.
Immediate
First correction window / مهلة المعالجة الأولى
The assigned site owner must correct routine findings within 24 hours and public-safety findings within the same shift wherever exposure exists.
24h
Level escalation / انتقال التصعيد
If the finding remains unresolved after the allowed window, the case must move to the next escalation level without waiting for the next weekly meeting.
Mandatory
Verification closure / إقفال التحقق
No item is treated as closed until the Quality Supervisor re-inspects, accepts evidence, and confirms that the same point does not present repeat exposure.
Control

2.3 Escalation Triggers That Must Be Applied

Trigger 1: Any public-safety exposure such as missing barrier, missing warning lighting, or unsafe open work zone must be escalated immediately on the same day.
Trigger 2: Any item not corrected within 24–48 hours must move up one level automatically.
Trigger 3: Any repeated finding on the same street, district, or code type within the same recovery cycle must be escalated to Project Manager level.
Trigger 4: Five or more open findings in one district, or a flat / worsening weekly trend, requires management review and a documented action reset.
Trigger 5: Any permit, reinstatement, or scope deviation not corrected within the agreed SLA must be escalated through the governance path without delay.
Trigger 6: Any unresolved item with potential STC visibility, municipality penalty impact, or client complaint risk must be flagged for senior review.

2.4 Corrective Actions & Responsible Parties

Actions are written as executable field controls. The responsible owner is the person accountable for completion; the Quality Supervisor is the verification owner for field evidence and pass/fail sign-off.

CategoryCorrective ActionPrimary ResponsibleVerification OwnerEscalation Owner
Signage failuresInstall compliant project boards and execution team / utility branding at each active site before start of work; replace missing permit signage within 24 hours; verify night visibility.Field Team LeadQuality SupervisorProject Manager
Site cleanupClear debris, stockpiles, and waste at end of each shift; restore site boundary and housekeeping before demobilization; close punch list same day.Field Team LeadQuality SupervisorProject Manager
Incorrect cable / civil installationCorrect trench reinstatement, asphalt cutting, and scope/permit deviations; stop any activity outside permit until re-approved; rework defective locations within 72 hours.Project ManagerQuality SupervisorField Team Lead
Protective cover / traffic safety control failuresDeploy compliant barriers, reflective warning devices, temporary protection, and traffic safety controls before excavation starts; replace damaged barriers immediately.Quality SupervisorField Team LeadProject Manager
Quality Supervisor Role – Concrete Weekly Duties
  • Start each day with a district priority route covering active and previously failed sites.
  • Verify physical closure with photo evidence, exact location reference, and code-by-code sign-off.
  • Stop work immediately where permit, barrier, warning-light, or public-safety controls are not in place.
  • Escalate any repeated finding on the same street or district within 24 hours to the Project Manager.
  • Issue a daily log listing closed items, rejected closure claims, and next-day reinspection points.

Inspection Protocol / بروتوكول التفتيش

Daily / يومي
Protective cover, signage, unsafe public exposure.
High Risk
Twice Weekly / مرتان أسبوعيًا
Cleanup and restoration progress by district.
Medium
Weekly / أسبوعي
Trend review, repeated violations, PM escalation.
Management
Re-inspection / إعادة تفتيش
Within 48 hours for any failed checkpoint.
Mandatory
Required evidence: before/after photo, district, exact violation text, responsible supervisor, and timestamp.

CAP Assessment & Certification

Integrated below Inspection Protocol / بروتوكول التفتيش
All panels below synchronize with the active dashboard filters.

1) Scenario-Based Assessment Questions

Grounded in real dataset examples

2) Practical Field Assessments

Mock inspections using CAP checkpoints and weekly status reporting

3) Competency Rubrics for Decision Authority

Boundary of independent approval vs mandatory escalation
Violation PatternSupervisor Can Approve IndependentlyEscalation TriggerRequired TimelineEscalate To

Decision Authority Rubric

Score each decision scenario against five tests: correct violation classification, correct risk rating, correct decision authority, correct CAP action, and correct evidence requirement. Passing threshold: 90% overall and 100% accuracy on safety-critical escalation cases.

Required Decision Scenario Bank

    Authority Rule

    A certified Quality Supervisor may close only what is fully corrected, evidenced, and within defined authority. Anything safety-critical, repeated, resource-blocked, permit-related, or beyond the CAP timeline must move up the escalation chain immediately.

    4) Passing Criteria & Remediation Pathways

    What “certification-ready” means

    Passing Thresholds

    Scenario-Based Questions≥ 80% accuracy on violation identification and corrective action
    Escalation Decisions100% accuracy on safety-critical escalation calls
    Field AssessmentComplete checkpoint coverage with zero critical omissions
    Decision Authority Rubric≥ 90% correct decisions across at least 15 scenarios
    Documentation QualityWeekly report complete, evidence-linked, time-bound, and owner-tagged

    Remediation Pathway

    1. Below threshold on any component: fail that component and assign targeted remediation.
    2. Attempt 1: repeat related module + mentor review + supervised field drill.
    3. Attempt 2: reassessment after cooling period with new mock scenario set.
    4. Maximum cycle: two re-attempts; then escalate to leadership for role decision.
    5. Mandatory retraining triggers: repeated escalation errors, poor evidence quality, missed safety exposure, or poor report discipline.

    Assessor Observation Criteria

    • Inspects methodically and in checkpoint order
    • Classifies violations without guessing
    • Links issue to CAP checkpoint and required timeline
    • Documents owner, deadline, evidence, and escalation flag
    • Communicates findings clearly to field team and PM chain
    Synchronized with current filter
